children.heading: Animal
children.label: 1 Animal
children.text: The term “animal” means any member of the animal kingdom (except a human).

children.heading: Article
children.label: 2 Article
children.text: The term “article” means any pest or disease or any material or tangible object that could harbor a pest or disease.

children.heading: Disease
children.label: 3 Disease
children.text: The term “disease” has the meaning given the term by the Secretary.

children.heading: Enter
children.label: 4 Enter
children.text: The term “enter” means to move into the commerce of the United States.

children.heading: Export
children.label: 5 Export
children.text: The term “export” means to move from a place within the territorial limits of the United States to a place outside the territorial limits of the United States.

children.heading: Facility
children.label: 6 Facility
children.text: The term “facility” means any structure.

children.heading: Import
children.label: 7 Import
children.text: The term “import” means to move from a place outside the territorial limits of the United States to a place within the territorial limits of the United States.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 8 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.label: A
children.text: between a place in a State and a place in another State, or between places within the same State but through any place outside that State; or

children.label: B
children.text: within the District of Columbia or any territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: Livestock
children.label: 10 Livestock
children.text: The term “livestock” means all farm-raised animals.

children.heading: Means of conveyance
children.label: 11 Means of conveyance
children.text: The term “means of conveyance” means any personal property used for or intended for use for the movement of any other personal property.

children.label: A
children.text: to carry, enter, import, mail, ship, or transport;

children.label: B
children.text: to aid, abet, cause, or induce carrying, entering, importing, mailing, shipping, or transporting;

children.label: C
children.text: to offer to carry, enter, import, mail, ship, or transport;

children.label: D
children.text: to receive in order to carry, enter, import, mail, ship, or transport;

children.label: E
children.text: to release into the environment; or

children.label: F
children.text: to allow any of the activities described in this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: A protozoan.

children.label: B
children.text: A plant.

children.label: C
children.text: A bacteria.

children.label: D
children.text: A fungus.

children.label: E
children.text: A virus or viroid.

children.label: F
children.text: An infectious agent or other pathogen.

children.label: G
children.text: An arthropod.

children.label: H
children.text: A parasite.

children.label: I
children.text: A prion.

children.label: J
children.text: A vector.

children.label: K
children.text: Any organism similar to or allied with any of the organisms described in this paragraph.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 14 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: State
children.label: 15 State
children.text: The term “State” means any of the States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, the Virgin Islands of the United States, or any territory or possession of the United States.

children.heading: This chapter
children.label: 16 This chapter
children.text: Except when used in this section, the term “this chapter” includes any regulation or order issued by the Secretary under the authority of this chapter.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 17 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means all of the States.

children.heading: Veterinary countermeasure
children.label: 18 Veterinary countermeasure
children.text: The term “veterinary countermeasure” means any biological product (including an animal vaccine or diagnostic), pharmaceutical product (including a therapeutic), non-pharmaceutical product (including a disinfectant), or other product or equipment to prevent, detect, respond to, or mitigate harm to public or animal health resulting from, animal pests or diseases.
